notes: All 136 tests pass (+8 new adapter tests). check_no_secrets.py exit 0 over qwen_adapters.py.
Scope matches files_to_touch exactly: tools/bridge/live/qwen_adapters.py and tests/live/test_qwen_adapters.py. No other files modified.

ENABLED_ADAPTERS in run_isolated_validation.py is not touched; test_enabled_adapters_stays_empty confirms this after import. The adapter cannot be activated via the CLI in the shared remote environment.

parse_qwen_result validates in this order: envelope shape, cost ceiling, review schema (Draft7Validator), reviewer == 'qwen', task_id match. The order is correct: schema validation before the explicit field checks.

test_wrong_reviewer_rejected constructs a schema-valid Claude reviewer payload (with allOf-required risk_assessment, human_review_required, notes fields) so the explicit reviewer guard is exercised rather than the schema check. This tests the right invariant.

CLI flags in build_qwen_adapter (--print --output-format json --json-schema --tools '' --no-session-persistence --disable-slash-commands --max-budget-usd) are assumed to mirror the Claude Code CLI interface. They are marked with a comment as a PC preflight item. The builder must run `qwen --help` and update the command tuple before the live run if the flags differ.
authentication_class='environment_secret' and environment_keys=('QWEN_API_KEY',) are correct: the API key is injected from the PC environment and never committed.
allowed_workspace_files defaults to () (no-write scope) — correct for reviewer role.
Independence caveat: same model built and reviewed. A second reviewer (Qwen on PC or human) should confirm before the first live credentialed run, particularly the CLI flag assumptions.
